recipes.title: Kabuli Pulao
recipes.description: Afghanistan's national dish — long-grain rice steamed with lamb, caramelised carrots, and raisins.
steps: Brown lamb in oil with sliced onions until deeply colored.
Add water to cover, salt, and char masala; simmer 1 hour until tender. Reserve broth.
Julienne carrots and saute with sugar in oil until caramelised; remove.
Plump raisins and slivered almonds briefly in the same oil.
Soak rice 30 minutes, then parboil in salted water to 70 percent done; drain.
Layer parboiled rice over the lamb in a heavy pot, scatter carrots, raisins, almonds, and cumin.
Drizzle with reserved broth and oil, cover tightly, and steam on lowest heat 25 minutes.

recipes.title: Mantu
recipes.description: Afghan steamed dumplings filled with spiced beef and onion, topped with yogurt and tomato lentil sauce.
steps: Knead flour with water and salt into a firm dough; rest 30 minutes.
Mix minced beef with finely chopped onion, salt, and pepper.
Cook split peas with tomato puree, oil, salt, and water into a thick lentil sauce.
Whisk yogurt with crushed garlic and salt.
Roll dough thinly and cut into squares.
Place a spoon of filling in each square and pinch into a small parcel; steam 25 minutes.
Plate with garlic yogurt below, dumplings on top, lentil sauce around, and a sprinkle of dried mint.

recipes.title: Bolani
recipes.description: Crisp pan-fried Afghan flatbread stuffed with mashed potatoes, leeks, or pumpkin and herbs.
steps: Knead flour, salt, water, and 1 tablespoon oil into a soft dough; rest 30 minutes.
Boil and mash potatoes; mix with finely chopped leeks, scallions, coriander, salt, and chili.
Divide dough into balls and roll each into a thin round.
Spread filling on half the round and fold over to form a half-moon.
Press the edges to seal, removing trapped air.
Pan fry in oil over medium heat until both sides are golden and crisp.
Slice and serve hot with garlic yogurt sauce.

recipes.title: Aushak
recipes.description: Afghan leek-filled dumplings topped with garlic yogurt and a spiced minced meat tomato sauce.
steps: Knead flour with water and salt into a firm dough; rest 30 minutes.
Finely chop leeks and scallions, salt them, and squeeze out excess moisture.
Brown minced beef with onion and tomato puree until thickened into a meat sauce.
Whisk yogurt with crushed garlic and salt.
Roll dough thinly and cut into rounds.
Place leek filling in each round, fold and seal into half-moons or circles.
Boil dumplings 5 minutes, drain, and plate with yogurt under, dumplings, meat sauce, and dried mint.

recipes.title: Korma E Murgh
recipes.description: Afghan chicken korma with caramelised onions, yogurt, and tomato, lightly spiced with char masala.
steps: Slice onions thinly and fry in oil until deep brown; reserve some for garnish.
Add minced garlic and ginger and saute briefly.
Add chicken pieces and brown lightly.
Stir in chopped tomato, turmeric, char masala, and salt; cook until tomato breaks down.
Whisk yogurt and stir into the pot over low heat.
Cover and simmer 30 minutes until chicken is tender.
Garnish with reserved onions and coriander; serve with naan.

recipes.title: Qabili Palau
recipes.description: Festive Afghan rice dish with lamb, raisins, and julienned carrots layered and steamed dum-style.
steps: Brown lamb pieces with onions in oil until deeply colored.
Cover with water, add salt and char masala, and simmer 1 hour until tender. Reserve broth.
Caramelise julienned carrots in oil with sugar until glossy.
Plump raisins and toast nuts briefly in the same oil.
Parboil rice in salted water until 70 percent done; drain.
Layer rice over the lamb, top with carrots, raisins, and nuts; drizzle reserved broth and oil.
Cover tightly and steam on lowest heat for 30 minutes; serve mounded on a platter.

recipes.title: Ashak
recipes.description: Spring Afghan dish of boiled gandana-stuffed pasta squares topped with garlic yogurt and meat sauce.
steps: Knead flour, water, and salt to a smooth dough; rest 30 minutes.
Saute chopped onion in oil, add minced lamb, brown, then add tomato puree and salt; simmer 20 minutes.
Finely chop gandana and salt to draw out moisture, then squeeze dry.
Roll dough very thin and cut into rounds; fill with gandana, fold, and crimp into half-moons.
Boil dumplings in salted water for 5 minutes; drain.
Whisk yogurt with crushed garlic and salt.
Plate yogurt below, ashak on top, meat sauce around, and a generous sprinkle of dried mint.

recipes.title: Sheer Yakh
recipes.description: Afghan rosewater and cardamom ice cream churned with pistachios, made traditionally with a salt-ice bucket.
steps: Whisk salep powder with a little cold milk to a smooth slurry.
Heat remaining milk and sugar until just steaming.
Whisk in the salep slurry and cook 10 minutes until thickened.
Stir in cream, rose water, and cardamom; cool completely.
Pour into an ice cream maker and churn until thick.
Fold in chopped pistachios.
Freeze at least 4 hours before serving in scoops.

recipes.title: Chapli Kebab
recipes.description: Pashtun-Afghan flat ground beef patties spiced with coriander seeds, pomegranate seeds, and tomato.
steps: Crush coriander and pomegranate seeds coarsely.
Mix minced beef with chopped onion, tomato, chilies, crushed seeds, salt, egg, and cornmeal.
Knead 5 minutes and rest the mixture for 20 minutes.
Shape into thin flat patties about a palm in size.
Heat oil in a wide pan over medium heat.
Fry patties 4 minutes per side, pressing lightly so they stay flat.
Drain on paper and serve with naan, sliced tomato, and lemon.
